economic_notes: First Nation holds interests in Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership (hotel in Little Current ON), UCCM Building Material Supply Company Ltd (16.67% interest), Mnidoo Mnising Power Limited Partnership (16.665% interest), Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth L.P. (0.5024% interest), and OFN Asset Management GP Corp. Robinson Huron Treaty Litigation Fund guarantee also noted.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022 (not 2024 as template default). Province is Ontario (Manitoulin Island). No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. Total government transfers estimated from segmented disclosure: ISC $3,895,052 + ISC Health $2,390 + Kinoomaadziwin Education Body $2,388,182 + OFNLP $446,374 + CMHC $81,523 + Ministry of Community and Social Services $557,846 + Ministry of Education $601,617 = $7,972,984 from segment; however income statement shows ISC $3,897,442 and Other $7,180,284 with deferred contributions beginning of year $1,163,256 included in total revenue of $12,240,982. OSR calculated as total revenue $12,240,982 minus ISC $3,897,442 minus other identifiable government transfers (CMHC $81,523, KEB $2,388,182, OFNLP $446,374, MCSS $557,846, MOE $601,617) = $4,268,008. Four equity-method investments treated as GBEs. No subsidiaries fully consolidated beyond the First Nation itself. COVID-19 was a significant program area. Funds held in trust by ISC $2,023 noted on balance sheet.
